result:
1. Per-route body limit config — add `config/body_size_limits.json` mapping `METHOD route_prefix → bytes`, default 2MB fallback. Entry `"POST /v1/media/upload-meta": 524288`. Keeps limit hot-reloadable via Argo without code redeploy; rollback = config revert.

2. `body_size_guard` middleware change — lookup per-route limit before emitting 413; today's middleware (pipeline step 4 in `src/edge/pipeline.ts`) uses single 2MB const. Keep it at step 4, BEFORE `auth_jwt`, since DoS vector is unauth'd. Reject via streaming size check, not after full buffering, else DoS still works.

3. 413 response shape — RFC 7807 problem+json per Section 2 rule 6: `type: /errors/payload-too-large`, `title`, `status: 413`, `detail: "Body exceeds 512KB for this route"`, `instance`, `request_id`. No internal hostnames, no library name, no stacktrace. No `retry_after` (not rate-limited).

4. Feature flag / env knob — gate new limit on `BODY_SIZE_UPLOAD_META_BYTES` env var read at boot, falling back to config file. Lets oncall bump to 2MB via Kustomize overlay without revert PR if legit partner breaks.

5. Pre-rollout traffic analysis — query Loki 7d for `route=/v1/media/upload-meta` body-size distribution; confirm p99.9 payload < 512KB on legit traffic. If not, raise limit or notify partners before shipping. Without this, rollout itself is the incident.

6. Unit tests — Jest in `src/edge/body_size_guard.test.ts`: (a) default route 2MB still passes 1.5MB, (b) `/v1/media/upload-meta` rejects 513KB with 413, (c) `/v1/media/upload-meta` passes 511KB, (d) error body matches Problem+JSON schema and omits internals.

7. Integration test — in `tests/integration/`, real Express+Redis+PG per Section 11, hit `POST /v1/media/upload-meta` with 600KB and 400KB payloads; assert 413 and 200/401 respectively. Reuses real stack since mock-DB is banned per 2025-Q3 incident.

8. Metrics — add `atlas_edge_body_size_rejected_total{route, method, limit_bytes}` counter; increment in middleware. Pre-existing `atlas_edge_requests_total{status_class="4xx"}` already captures it but lacks size-vs-limit signal. 15s scrape, dashboard panel on main Atlas board.

9. Always-trace — add `POST /v1/media/upload-meta` to `config/always_trace_routes.json` for the rollout window so 100% of 413s have a trace for abuse forensics + false-positive diagnosis. Remove after 2-week soak.

10. Alert — new Alertmanager rule: if `atlas_edge_body_size_rejected_total{route="/v1/media/upload-meta"}` 5-min rate spikes >3× pre-rollout baseline for legit-identified users (JWT-verified subset), page SEV3 to `#oncall-platform`. Filters out the attacker traffic we expect to reject.

11. Logging — on rejection log `request_id`, `trusted_client_ip`, `route`, `content_length`, `limit_bytes`. Never log body content (Section 11). `X-Api-Key` / `Authorization` already redacted by existing pipeline; confirm `pii_redact` covers upload-meta fields (`filename`, possibly `email`).

12. CODEOWNERS gap — Section 6 lists `auth_jwt.ts`, `rate_limit.ts`, `header_sanitize.ts`, `trusted_proxies.json` as human-approval gated, but NOT `body_size_guard.ts` or the new config. Add both to CODEOWNERS this PR; limit change is a security-adjacent trust-boundary decision.

13. Rollout sequence — canary `us-east-1` 5% for 30 min → full us-east-1 → 10 min soak → `eu-west-1` full → 10 min → `ap-southeast-1` full, per Section 6. Watch body_size_rejected delta and 4xx rate per region between steps. Auto-rollback on SEV1 already wired.

14. Rollback plan — two tiers: (a) config revert (set limit back to 2MB) via Argo sync of overlay, ~2-3 min propagation, no rebuild; (b) code revert via `git revert` on `main` if middleware logic itself regressed. Document both in PR description. SEV1 triggers (a) automatically per Section 6.

15. Communication — (a) partner API consumers of `/v1/media/upload-meta`: email + changelog entry 14 days pre-rollout citing new 512KB cap and 413 behavior; (b) `#oncall-platform` announcement at T-24h and T-0; (c) security oncall notified since driver is active abuse (may want to correlate attacker IPs pre/post); (d) update public API docs page for the route.

16. Abuse-source handling — dropping bodies at edge just forces attacker to retry; pair rollout with global-LB IP ban list update for the abusing ASNs/IPs already identified, else 413 floods Redis+HAProxy. Coordinate with security oncall, not unilateral.

17. Bundle-size + lint gates — change is tiny, but confirm <5% bundle delta (Section 6) and no new `no-direct-xff-read` / `no-unbounded-redis-key` triggers introduced by touching middleware.
